title: Mailchimp Campaign
description: A Mailchimp email marketing campaign. Represents a single email send or series of emails targeted at a list or segment of subscribers. Campaigns can be regular, plain-text, A/B split, RSS-driven, or multivariate.

id.description: A string that uniquely identifies this campaign.

web_id.description: The ID used in the Mailchimp web application.

parent_campaign_id.description: If this campaign is the child of another campaign, this identifies the parent campaign. For example, for RSS or Automation children.

create_time.description: The date and time the campaign was created in ISO 8601 format.

archive_url.description: The link to the campaign's archive version.

long_archive_url.description: The original link to the campaign's archive version.

status.description: The current status of the campaign.

emails_sent.description: The total number of emails sent for this campaign.

send_time.description: The date and time a campaign was sent in ISO 8601 format.

content_type.description: How the campaign's content is put together.

needs_block_refresh.description: Deprecated. Whether the campaign needs its blocks refreshed by opening the web-based campaign editor. Will always return false.

resendable.description: Whether the campaign qualifies to be resent to non-openers.

list_is_active.description: The status of the list used, namely if it's deleted or disabled.

segment_text.description: A description of the segment used for the campaign, formatted as HTML.

recipient_count.description: Count of the recipients on the associated list.

segment_opts.description: Segmentation options for targeting a subset of the list.

saved_segment_id.description: The ID for an existing saved segment.

prebuilt_segment_id.description: The prebuilt segment ID, if a prebuilt segment has been designated.

conditions.description: An array of segment condition objects.

settings.description: The settings for the campaign, including subject line, from name, and reply-to address.

subject_line.description: The subject line for the campaign.

preview_text.description: The preview text for the campaign, shown in email client inboxes.

from_name.description: The 'from' name on the campaign (not an email address).

reply_to.description: The reply-to email address for the campaign.

use_conversation.description: Use Mailchimp Conversation feature to manage out-of-office replies.

to_name.description: The campaign's custom 'To' name. Typically the first name merge field.

folder_id.description: If the campaign is listed in a folder, the ID for that folder.

authenticate.description: Whether Mailchimp authenticated the campaign. Defaults to true.

auto_footer.description: Automatically append Mailchimp's default footer to the campaign.

inline_css.description: Automatically inline the CSS included with the campaign content.

auto_tweet.description: Automatically tweet a link to the campaign archive page when the campaign is sent.

fb_comments.description: Allows Facebook comments on the campaign (also determines determine the text of the Facebook post).

template_id.description: The ID for the template used in this campaign.

tracking.description: The tracking options for a campaign.

opens.description: Whether to track opens. Defaults to true.

html_clicks.description: Whether to track clicks in the HTML version of the campaign. Defaults to true.

text_clicks.description: Whether to track clicks in the plain-text version of the campaign. Defaults to true.

google_analytics.description: The custom slug for Google Analytics tracking (max 50 bytes).

report_summary.description: For sent campaigns, a summary of opens, clicks, and e-commerce data.

opens.description: The total number of opens for a campaign.

open_rate.description: The number of unique opens divided by the total number of successful deliveries.

clicks.description: The total number of clicks for a campaign.

click_rate.description: The number of unique clicks divided by the total number of successful deliveries.

total_orders.description: The total orders for a campaign.

total_spent.description: The total spent for a campaign.

total_revenue.description: The total revenue for a campaign.

delivery_status.description: Updates on campaigns in the process of sending.

enabled.description: Whether Campaign Delivery Status is enabled for this account and target campaign.

can_cancel.description: Whether a campaign send can be canceled.

status.description: The current state of a campaign delivery.

emails_sent.description: The total number of emails confirmed sent for this campaign so far.

emails_canceled.description: The total number of emails canceled for this campaign.
